title: Pagination in fabio
summary: fabio handles pagination via --all (auto-fetch all pages), --continuation-token (resume), and --limit (truncate). Agents rarely need to paginate manually.

fabio_behavior.default: Without flags, fabio returns the first page from the API (typically 100-5000 items depending on endpoint).
fabio_behavior.all_pages: --all fetches all pages automatically by following continuationToken links.
fabio_behavior.client_side_limit: --limit N truncates the result to N items (applied after pagination).

output_envelope.note: continuationToken is only present when there are more pages AND --all was not used

what_agents_should_do: Use --all when you need the complete list (workspaces, items, tables)
Use --limit for quick sampling or when you only need a few results
Check for 'continuationToken' in output if you didn't use --all — more data exists
Use --query with JMESPath to filter/project after fetching (e.g., --query "data[?type=='Lakehouse']")

relevant_flags.--all: Fetch all pages automatically (may be slow for large tenants)
